rationale: The cited paper is used in the conceptual argument as direct evidence that consumer characteristics (gender) can increase social considerations and cooperative pricing in participative pricing contexts. The citing authors then explicitly adapt a validated manipulation from Santana and Morwitz (2021) (community-oriented platform framing) in Study 5 to test their own mechanism. Because it contributes to both hypothesis framing and experimental design, this is more than a passing background citation.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Santana and Morwitz (2021) by moving from gender-based differences in PWYW behavior to payment-method cues as a driver of cooperative pricing and reduced WTA-WTP gaps. It also borrows and applies a manipulation from the cited paper to operationalize social transaction context in its mechanism tests.
